name: Western Union
description_short: Western Union at 3800 Broadway Blvd, Kansas City, MO 64111 in the Valentine neighborhood is Mon-Fri 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM. Google reviewers rate the branch 3.6 stars across 14 reviews. Call +1 816-471-2274.
description_long:
Western Union is a long-established money transfer service founded in 1851 that has evolved into a global financial services network. The company operates primarily through agent locations—partnerships with retail stores, grocery chains, banks, and Walmart—rather than as a standalone storefront chain. This distributed model gives Western Union significant reach across the United States, making their services accessible to millions of consumers. The company emphasizes convenience, speed, and reliability as core pillars of their business model.

Western Union's service portfolio centers on in-person financial transactions. Customers can send domestic and international money transfers at agent locations, with the option to initiate transfers online or via mobile app and complete payment in cash at the counter. The company also offers bill payment services for utilities, rent, and car payments at participating locations. Money orders function as alternatives to checks, and cash pickup allows recipients to collect funds sent by others using a Money Transfer Control Number (MTCN). Additionally, Western Union offers prepaid Visa cards as an alternative to cash pickup for receiving funds.

What distinguishes Western Union is its extensive historical credibility—operating for over 170 years—combined with its widespread agent network embedded in everyday retail and banking environments. The company emphasizes security through encryption and fraud prevention measures. They offer flexibility in payment methods (cash, debit, and select credit card locations) and the ability to start transactions online to save time at physical locations. International transfers include automatic currency conversion at time-of-transfer rates. The MTCN tracking system provides transparency and accountability for both senders and recipients.

However, Western Union operates in a competitive landscape where digital money transfer services (Venmo, PayPal, Square Cash) have captured significant market share, particularly for domestic transfers among younger consumers. The company does not provide currency exchange services at agent locations—only transfer services. Pricing varies significantly by transfer method, destination, and payment option, potentially making in-person transactions more expensive than online alternatives. The service remains most valuable for cash-based senders and recipients without bank accounts or for international transfers where local currency delivery is essential.
pros: Extensive agent network across US with locations in Walmart, grocery chains, banks, and retail stores for convenient access
Long operational history since 1851 provides credibility and established fraud prevention infrastructure
Offers both domestic and international money transfers with automatic currency conversion for recipients
Hybrid online-to-offline model allows customers to initiate transfers online or via app and pay cash in-store to save time
Multiple service options including bill payments, money orders, cash pickup, and prepaid card delivery
Money Transfer Control Number (MTCN) tracking system enables transparency and confirms fund arrival timing
No minimum balance or credit check required for prepaid card receipt option
cons: Fees and exchange rates are not transparent upfront on the main website; pricing varies significantly by method and destination
Does not provide in-person currency exchange services, limiting value for international travelers
Operating hours vary by agent location with no unified schedule, creating potential inconvenience
In-person transactions may cost more than online-only alternatives, though pricing calculator is available
Service depends on partner locations' availability and operational status; agent network limitations affect accessibility in rural areas
best_for: Unbanked or underbanked consumers who need cash-based money transfer and bill payment services
International remittance senders and recipients who need funds delivered in local currency
Customers without smartphone access or digital banking who rely on in-person financial services
Individuals sending emergency funds domestically who need same-day or rapid transfer capability
diagnosis: Western Union is best for cash-based consumers, international remittance senders/recipients, and unbanked individuals who need reliable in-person money transfer and bill payment services. The main caveat is that pricing is variable and often higher than digital alternatives, and the service depends on partner location availability rather than dedicated storefronts.
services: In-person domestic money transfers at agent locations
International money transfers with currency conversion
Bill payment services (utilities, rent, car payments) at participating locations
Cash pickup for funds sent via MTCN
Western Union Prepaid Visa Card for receiving money
Online transfer initiation with in-store cash payment
Retail and grocery store agent location network
Walmart-specific money transfer and cash pickup services
